steps.title: Simplify the first equation to find its slope
steps.content:
Start with $$48x - 64y = 48y + 24$$. Collect all $y$ terms on the left and constants on the right:
$$48x - 64y - 48y = 24$$
$$48x - 112y = 24$$

Divide by 16 to simplify:
$$3x - 7y = \frac{3}{2}$$

Solve for $y$ to get slope-intercept form:
$$y = \frac{3}{7}x - \frac{3}{14}$$

The slope of this line is $\frac{3}{7}$.

steps.title: Identify the condition for no solution
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the lines are parallel—they have the same slope but different $y$-intercepts. Since equation 1 has slope $\frac{3}{7}$, equation 2 must also have slope $\frac{3}{7}$ for the lines to be parallel.

steps.title: Solve for r by matching slopes
steps.content:
Rearrange the second equation to isolate $y$:
$$ry = -12x + \frac{1}{8}$$

Divide both sides by $r$:
$$y = -\frac{12}{r}x + \frac{1}{8r}$$

The slope of this line is $-\frac{12}{r}$. For parallel lines, set this equal to the slope from equation 1:
$$-\frac{12}{r} = \frac{3}{7}$$

Cross-multiply and simplify:
$$-12 \cdot 7 = 3r$$
$$-84 = 3r$$
$$r = -28$$
